questions.question: Which country made history by becoming the second-smallest country to qualify for the FIFA World Cup after Iceland?
questions.explanation: Recently, Cape Verde made history by becoming the second-smallest country, after Iceland in 2018, to qualify for the FIFA World Cup. In contrast, India failed to qualify for the 2027 Asian Cup after losing to world-ranked 158th Singapore in Goa. Cape Verde, also known as Cabo Verde, is an archipelago of 10 islands in the Atlantic Ocean. It lies about 620 kilometres off the west coast of Africa, named after Cape Verde in Senegal.

questions.question: Who won the 2025 Major League Soccer (MLS) Golden Boot?
questions.explanation: Lionel Messi won the 2025 Major League Soccer (MLS) Golden Boot with 29 goals and 19 assists for Inter Miami CF. This is Messi’s second full season with Inter Miami and he is Miami’s first Golden Boot winner. He outperformed Denis Bouanga and Sam Surridge to claim the award. Messi became the first Argentine to win the MLS Golden Boot since Taty Castellanos in 2021.

questions.question: India won which medal in Hockey at the Sultan of Johor Cup 2025 in Malaysia?
questions.explanation: India won the silver medal in Hockey at the Sultan of Johor Cup 2025 in Malaysia. India lost narrowly to Australia 2-1 in the final Australia’s Ian Grobbelaar scored first in the 13th minute. India’s Anmol Ekka equalized at the 17th minute, making it 1-1 at half-time. Ian Grobbelaar scored again in the 59th minute, securing Australia’s win. This is India’s fifth runners-up finish at the Sultan of Johor Cup. India had won bronze medals in the previous two editions. Australia ended their three consecutive final losses streak, avenging their 2022 defeat to India.

questions.question: Which Olympic javelin champion was conferred the honorary rank of Lieutenant Colonel in the Territorial Army on 22 October 2025?
questions.explanation: On 22 October 2025, Olympic javelin champion Neeraj Chopra was awarded the honorary rank of Lieutenant Colonel in the Territorial Army. The ceremony was held in New Delhi, attended by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh and Army Chief General Upendra Dwivedi. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh praised Neeraj as a symbol of perseverance, patriotism, and excellence, inspiring sports and Armed Forces communities. Neeraj Chopra joined the Indian Army in 2016 as a Naib Subedar and later became Subedar Major. He won the historic Olympic gold medal at Tokyo 2020 in javelin throw.

questions.question: Which country won gold medals in both Under-18 Boys’ and Under-18 Girls’ Kabaddi events at the 2025 Asian Youth Games?
questions.explanation: Recently, India achieved a kabaddi double gold at the 2025 Asian Youth Games in Bahrain. Both Under-18 boys’ and girls’ teams remained unbeaten throughout the tournament. The girls’ team defeated Iran 75-21 in the final — the biggest victory margin in a Youth Games kabaddi final since 2013. Raider Neha Patel scored 28 points, and defender Riya Singh led a strong defence. India’s girls scored 312 points and conceded only 89 in five matches. The boys’ team beat Iran 35-32 in a close final at Isa Sports City, Riffa. The clean sweep reaffirmed India’s dominance as Asia’s youth kabaddi powerhouse and boosted its overall medal tally.

questions.question: Rohan Bopanna, who recently announced his retirement, is associated with which sports?
questions.explanation: Tennis veteran Rohan Bopanna announced his retirement at the age of 45. His final match was at the Paris Masters 1000, where he partnered with Alexander Bublik. In 2024, he became the oldest player to win a Men’s Doubles Grand Slam at the Australian Open. He also became the oldest Association of Tennis Professionals (ATP) 1000 winner at the Miami Open 2024. He achieved World Number 1 ranking in doubles in 2024. Throughout his career, he won 26 doubles titles, including six ATP Masters 1000 titles.

questions.question: Kushal Dalal, who recently won the GT Open Indoor World Series title, is associated with which sport?
questions.explanation: Indian archer Kushal Dalal won the GT Open Indoor World Series title in the men’s compound category in Strassen, Luxembourg. He clinched gold after defeating former world No. 1 Stephen Hansen (USA) in a rare double shoot-off. Ganesh Mani Ratnam Thirumuru of India won bronze in the Under-21 men’s compound category.

questions.question: Which state/UT is the host of 69th National School Games U-19 Table Tennis Tournament?
questions.explanation: Jammu & Kashmir is hosting the 69th National School Games Under-19 Table Tennis Tournament. Around 400 players from 34 States, Union Territories, and organisations across India are participating. The tournament promises a showcase of skill, sportsmanship, and competitive spirit among young players.

questions.question: Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy 2025 is associated with which sports?
questions.explanation: India’s top men’s T20 domestic cricket tournament, Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy 2025, started from 27 November. It was started in 2007. A total of 38 teams participated in 2025. It is a Twenty20 championship organized by the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI). The tournament is named after former Indian Test cricketer Syed Mushtaq Ali.

questions.question: India won which medal in hockey at the Sultan Azlan Shah Cup 2025?
questions.explanation: India won the silver medal in hockey at the Sultan Azlan Shah Cup 2025 in Ipoh, Malaysia. Belgium defeated India 1-0, with Thibeau Stockbroekx scoring the only goal. This was Belgium’s first trophy in their second appearance at the tournament. India returned after a six-year gap and finished as runners-up for the second consecutive time, having lost the 2019 final to Republic of Korea. India, with five titles, remains the second-most successful team, after Australia who have 10 wins.
